methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series A → next-stage step-up multiplier (2.0x) to $23M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 4 same-sector + same/adjacent-stage peers in Chips & Semiconductors. Range: $500M - $5.0B.

methods.name: Public-Comp Anchoring
methods.skip_reason: target revenue not on file (private companies rarely disclose)
methods.rationale: Public Semiconductors P/S median is 20.3x across 3 tickers. Cannot apply without target's revenue.

private.name: Substrate
private.rationale: Same sector (Chips & Semiconductors), same stage (Series A), post-money $1B+

private.name: Rain AI
private.rationale: Same sector (Chips & Semiconductors), same stage (Series A), post-money $500M

data.narrative:
Voyant Photonics is a Series A semiconductor company founded by Chris Phare and Steven Miller that has raised $23M to date. The valuation is anchored against a peer universe of 8 private companies and 3 public semiconductor comparables, including Etched, Substrate, Rain AI, Positron AI, and public tickers ALAB, NVDA, and AMD.

Two valuation methods triangulate a credible range. Stage-progression applies a 2.0x step-up multiplier to the $23M raised, implying $46M. Peer-median valuation across four same-sector and adjacent-stage private companies—ranging from $500M (Rain AI, Series A) to $5.0B (Etched, Series B)—yields a $1.0B median. Public-comp anchoring cannot be applied without disclosed revenue figures. The three public semiconductor comparables trade at a 20.3x median price-to-sales multiple, but Voyant's private revenues are unavailable.

The two applicable methods produce materially divergent signals. Stage-progression suggests $46M; peer-median suggests $1.0B. The triangulated range spans $39M (low) to $1.1B (high), with a median of $523M. This wide spread reflects genuine uncertainty: the peer cohort contains significant heterogeneity in stage progression and business maturity, and stage-progression methodology alone may underweight frontier-sector valuation momentum. Key risks to the range include revenue trajectory post-Series A, technical differentiation relative to Substrate and Rain AI, and whether the current peer cohort remains representative as the chip-design landscape evolves.
